visual_features.overall_style: Clean, minimalist game-portal layout with a strong cinematic hero banner. Uses a mostly monochrome palette with subtle warm tones from the in‑game artwork. The style feels modern and understated, prioritizing readability and structure over flashy UI elements.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: 1) Brand/logo and dark hero banner dominate the top. 2) Primary navigation (Notice, Item Book, language switch) sits over the hero but with low visual weight. 3) Centered tab-like filters (Notice / Event) provide a clear secondary focal point. 4) The notice list/table is the main functional area; column headers are slightly stronger than row text, and the Subject column is visually dominant by width. 5) Dates and writer names are low-emphasis, small, right-aligned text. Hierarchy is achieved mainly through size, contrast (dark on light), and spacing rather than color.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Large margins around the main content column and generous vertical spacing between the hero area, tab controls, and the notice table. Inside the table, rows are relatively compact but still have comfortable line height. There is substantial white background around all elements, creating a calm, breathable layout despite a long list of items.
unique_elements: Cinematic in-game hero banner spanning full width with a dark overlay, anchoring the otherwise minimal page.
Very light, almost borderless table styling that relies on subtle horizontal rules and alignment rather than heavy borders.
Center-aligned tab controls (Notice / Event) placed in the whitespace between hero and content, visually acting as a bridge between them.
Tiny grey label badges ('H') next to some subjects, visually marking special posts without adding much color noise.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Centered, fixed-width content column (approximately 1080–1200px) likely based on a 12-column grid with large outer margins.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width hero header with background image, followed by a centered single-column layout containing tabs and a table-based content list.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top global header with logo, navigation, and language switch overlaid on the hero image; below that, a large white content area that starts with a small meta-info line ('Total 34 / 1 page'), then filter tabs (Notice / Event), and then a structured notice table. The page is essentially one main section (news list) framed by the hero banner.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Logo left, language toggle right in the top bar; main navigation left-aligned within the hero region. Content column is center-aligned within the viewport. Inside the table, columns use mixed alignment: left for 'No.' and 'Subject', right for 'Writer' and 'Date'. Tab controls are horizontally centered.
responsive_hints: Large horizontal margins suggest the central column would simply scale down on smaller viewports.
Table columns are cleanly separated, hinting at a potential responsive pattern where columns might stack or collapse (e.g., hiding Writer on mobile).
Hero banner uses background image cropping; on mobile this likely becomes taller or more tightly cropped without altering layout logic.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: full-width hero header with logo/nav overlay → large white gap → centered tab controls (Notice / Event) rendered as two adjacent bordered buttons → small status line for total posts → full-width notice list implemented as a table with one header row and multiple body rows. Each row runs left to right: narrow index column, wide subject column with optional badges, narrow writer column, narrow right-aligned date.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Roughly the top 25–30% of the viewport is occupied by the dark hero banner. The remaining area is dominated by the white content region, where the notice table occupies most of the vertical space. Tabs and meta text take relatively little space but are surrounded by generous vertical padding (about 24–40px above and below), emphasizing section breaks. Within the table, header row height is slightly larger than body rows; the Subject column visually takes about 60–65% of the table width, with remaining columns sharing the rest.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately low density. There are many rows, but the design stays airy thanks to ample whitespace surrounding the table and subtle row separation. Row content itself is single-line and compact, but overall layout feels spacious, closer to an editorial/news list than a data-heavy dashboard.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary horizontal flow across the top: eye starts at the REBIRTH logo on the left, scans across the dark hero banner to the right where the character stands, then up/right to the language toggle. From there, the large white region below pulls the eye downward to the centered Notice/Event tabs, then down again to the table header and sequentially through the notice rows. The subject column creates a strong vertical reading line toward the center-left of the page.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Rectangular buttons with subtle rounding (approximately 4px radius), white background, thin light-grey border (#E5E5E5), and medium-grey text. No heavy shadows; flat design with high contrast against the white page because of the border and spacing. Default state appears understated; active state is likely represented by darker text, slightly bolder border, or a soft background tint rather than a strong color fill.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented either as .

ui_patterns.visual_description: The page does not use separate cards; the table behaves as a large, minimal card. Each notice row functions as a flat strip with a white background, single-pixel horizontal separators in very light grey, and no shadows. Hover state would likely be a subtle background tint or underline on the subject text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Card-like rows implemented as <tr> within a <table>. Each row contains multiple <td> cells (No., Subject, Writer, Date). Alternately, on mobile this could be converted to <div class='notice-row'> with nested <span> elements for each field.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit form fields are visible in the captured area. Given the aesthetic, expected form controls would be full-width or column-width rectangular inputs with 4px radius, 1px light grey borders, white background, and dark grey text. Labels would likely be above the inputs, left-aligned, with small grey helper text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical pattern would be stacked vertical forms using <form> with <label> above each <input> or <select>, grouped into blocks. Validation states would probably rely on border-color changes and small inline messages below the input.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Top navigation is horizontal, minimal text links (e.g., 'Notice', 'Item Book') on the left inside the hero header area. Text is white or light grey over the dark background. Active state is likely indicated by stronger contrast or underline. Language toggle on the right (‘KO | EN’) is small, uppercase, with a vertical bar separator, also flat with no button chrome.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Likely implemented as a simple
    list styled horizontally:
    • Notice
    • Item Book
    . Language toggle probably uses two elements within a flex container, separated by a pseudo-element or span acting as a divider.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Notice list is a classic bulletin-board/table style. Each row is single-line, relatively low height, with light-grey horizontal separators and no vertical grid lines. The Subject cell contains the main link, optional small grey label badges inside rounded rectangles, and truncated titles with ellipsis. Numbers and meta information are dimmer and smaller, supporting the main title link.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Structurally a with for headers and for items. Each row: . Could also be converted to flexbox-based media rows for responsive layouts.

heading.visual_style: Logotype is a custom, bold geometric sans with sharp edges. In-page headings and labels appear to be a clean sans-serif with moderate width and relatively neutral character.

body.visual_style: Neutral, readable sans-serif with moderate x-height and low contrast, optimized for UI rather than display.

spacing_system.component_padding: Tab buttons and table cells appear to use approximately 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding, aligning to the 8px base.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (hero to tabs, tabs to table) are separated by roughly 32–48px of vertical space, creating clear structural breaks.

interaction_hints: Tab buttons (Notice/Event) likely change background or border color on hover and show a stronger active state, possibly with bolder text.
Table rows probably highlight on hover with a light background tint, and the Subject text link may gain an underline or color shift.
Language toggle and top navigation links almost certainly use a simple color or opacity change on hover.
Any transitions are likely subtle (150–250ms ease-in-out) for hover background and color changes, maintaining the minimal, non-flashy feel.
